cards.back: A type of machine learning where the algorithm is trained on labeled data to learn a mapping between input data and output labels.
A type of machine learning where the algorithm is trained on unlabeled data to identify patterns or structure.
When a model is too complex and performs well on the training data but poorly on new, unseen data, resulting in poor generalization.
When a model is too simple and fails to capture the underlying patterns in the data, resulting in poor performance on both training and test data.
The tradeoff between a model's bias (average error) and variance (model's sensitivity to random fluctuations), with a balance between the two resulting in the best generalization.
